text: Nocte os meum perforatur doloribus, et qui me comedunt, non dormiunt.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: In multitudine eorum consumitur vestimentum meum, et quasi capitio tunicæ succinxerunt me.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Comparatus sum luto, et assimilatus sum favillæ et cineri.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Clamo ad te, et non exaudis me : sto, et non respicis me.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Mutatus es mihi in crudelem, et in duritia manus tuæ adversaris mihi.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Elevasti me, et quasi super ventum ponens ; elisisti me valide.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Scio quia morti trades me, ubi constituta est domus omni viventi.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Verumtamen non ad consumptionem eorum emittis manum tuam : et si corruerint, ipse salvabis.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Flebam quondam super eo qui afflictus erat, et compatiebatur anima mea pauperi.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Expectabam bona, et venerunt mihi mala : præstolabar lucem, et eruperunt tenebræ.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Interiora mea efferbuerunt absque ulla requie : prævenerunt me dies afflictionis.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Mœrens incedebam sine furore ; consurgens, in turba clamabam.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Frater fui draconum, et socius struthionum.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Cutis mea denigrata est super me, et ossa mea aruerunt præ caumate.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Versa est in luctum cithara mea, et organum meum in vocem flentium.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Pepigi fœdus cum oculis meis, ut ne cogitarem quidem de virgine.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Quam enim partem haberet in me Deus desuper, et hæreditatem Omnipotens de excelsis ?

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Numquid non perditio est iniquo, et alienatio operantibus injustitiam ?

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Nonne ipse considerat vias meas, et cunctos gressus meos dinumerat ?

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Si ambulavi in vanitate, et festinavit in dolo pes meus,

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: appendat me in statera justa, et sciat Deus simplicitatem meam.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Si declinavit gressus meus de via, et si secutum est oculos meos cor meum, et si manibus meis adhæsit macula,

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: seram, et alius comedat, et progenies mea eradicetur.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Si deceptum est cor meum super muliere, et si ad ostium amici mei insidiatus sum,

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: scortum alterius sit uxor mea, et super illam incurventur alii.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Hoc enim nefas est, et iniquitas maxima.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Ignis est usque ad perditionem devorans, et omnia eradicans genimina.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Si contempsi subire judicium cum servo meo et ancilla mea, cum disceptarent adversum me :

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: quid enim faciam cum surrexerit ad judicandum Deus ? et cum quæsierit, quid respondebo illi ?

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Numquid non in utero fecit me, qui et illum operatus est, et formavit me in vulva unus ?

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Si negavi quod volebant pauperibus, et oculos viduæ expectare feci ;

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: si comedi buccellam meam solus, et non comedit pupillus ex ea

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: (quia ab infantia mea crevit mecum miseratio, et de utero matris meæ egressa est mecum) ;

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: si despexi pereuntem, eo quod non habuerit indumentum, et absque operimento pauperem ;

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: si non benedixerunt mihi latera ejus, et de velleribus ovium mearum calefactus est ;

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: si levavi super pupillum manum meam, etiam cum viderem me in porta superiorem :

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: humerus meus a junctura sua cadat, et brachium meum cum suis ossibus confringatur.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Semper enim quasi tumentes super me fluctus timui Deum, et pondus ejus ferre non potui.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Si putavi aurum robur meum, et obrizo dixi : Fiducia mea ;

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: si lætatus sum super multis divitiis meis, et quia plurima reperit manus mea ;

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: si vidi solem cum fulgeret, et lunam incedentem clare,

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: et lætatum est in abscondito cor meum, et osculatus sum manum meam ore meo :

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: quæ est iniquitas maxima, et negatio contra Deum altissimum.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Si gavisus sum ad ruinam ejus qui me oderat, et exsultavi quod invenisset eum malum :

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: non enim dedi ad peccandum guttur meum, ut expeterem maledicens animam ejus.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Si non dixerunt viri tabernaculi mei : Quis det de carnibus ejus, ut saturemur ?

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: foris non mansit peregrinus : ostium meum viatori patuit.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Si abscondi quasi homo peccatum meum, et celavi in sinu meo iniquitatem meam ;

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: si expavi ad multitudinem nimiam, et despectio propinquorum terruit me : et non magis tacui, nec egressus sum ostium.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Quis mihi tribuat auditorem, ut desiderium meum audiat Omnipotens, et librum scribat ipse qui judicat,

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: ut in humero meo portem illum, et circumdem illum quasi coronam mihi ?

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Per singulos gradus meos pronuntiabo illum, et quasi principi offeram eum.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Si adversum me terra mea clamat, et cum ipsa sulci ejus deflent :

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: si fructus ejus comedi absque pecunia, et animam agricolarum ejus afflixi :

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: pro frumento oriatur mihi tribulus, et pro hordeo spina. Finita sunt verba Job.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Omiserunt autem tres viri isti respondere Job, eo quod justus sibi videretur.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Et iratus indignatusque est Eliu filius Barachel Buzites, de cognatione Ram : iratus est autem adversum Job, eo quod justum se esse diceret coram Deo.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Porro adversum amicos ejus indignatus est, eo quod non invenissent responsionem rationabilem, sed tantummodo condemnassent Job.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Igitur Eliu expectavit Job loquentem, eo quod seniores essent qui loquebantur.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Cum autem vidisset quod tres respondere non potuissent, iratus est vehementer.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Respondensque Eliu filius Barachel Buzites, dixit : Junior sum tempore, vos autem antiquiores : idcirco, demisso capite, veritus sum vobis indicare meam sententiam.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Sperabam enim quod ætas prolixior loqueretur, et annorum multitudo doceret sapientiam.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Sed, ut video, spiritus est in hominibus, et inspiratio Omnipotentis dat intelligentiam.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Non sunt longævi sapientes, nec senes intelligunt judicium.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Ideo dicam : Audite me : ostendam vobis etiam ego meam sapientiam.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Expectavi enim sermones vestros ; audivi prudentiam vestram, donec disceptaremini sermonibus ;

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: et donec putabam vos aliquid dicere, considerabam : sed, ut video, non est qui possit arguere Job, et respondere ex vobis sermonibus ejus.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Ne forte dicatis : Invenimus sapientiam : Deus projecit eum, non homo.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Nihil locutus est mihi : et ego non secundum sermones vestros respondebo illi.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Extimuerunt, nec responderunt ultra, abstuleruntque a se eloquia.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Quoniam igitur expectavi, et non sunt locuti : steterunt, nec ultra responderunt :

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: respondebo et ego partem meam, et ostendam scientiam meam.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Plenus sum enim sermonibus, et coarctat me spiritus uteri mei.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: En venter meus quasi mustum absque spiraculo, quod lagunculas novas disrumpit.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Loquar, et respirabo paululum : aperiam labia mea, et respondebo.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Non accipiam personam viri, et Deum homini non æquabo.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Nescio enim quamdiu subsistam, et si post modicum tollat me factor meus.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Audi igitur, Job, eloquia mea, et omnes sermones meos ausculta.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Ecce aperui os meum : loquatur lingua mea in faucibus meis.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Simplici corde meo sermones mei, et sententiam puram labia mea loquentur.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Spiritus Dei fecit me, et spiraculum Omnipotentis vivificavit me.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Si potes, responde mihi, et adversus faciem meam consiste.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Ecce, et me sicut et te fecit Deus, et de eodem luto ego quoque formatus sum.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Verumtamen miraculum meum non te terreat, et eloquentia mea non sit tibi gravis.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Dixisti ergo in auribus meis, et vocem verborum tuorum audivi :

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Mundus sum ego, et absque delicto : immaculatus, et non est iniquitas in me.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Quia querelas in me reperit, ideo arbitratus est me inimicum sibi.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Posuit in nervo pedes meos ; custodivit omnes semitas meas.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Hoc est ergo in quo non es justificatus : respondebo tibi, quia major sit Deus homine.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Adversus eum contendis, quod non ad omnia verba responderit tibi ?

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Semel loquitur Deus, et secundo idipsum non repetit.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Per somnium, in visione nocturna, quando irruit sopor super homines, et dormiunt in lectulo,

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: tunc aperit aures virorum, et erudiens eos instruit disciplina,

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: ut avertat hominem ab his quæ facit, et liberet eum de superbia,

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: eruens animam ejus a corruptione, et vitam illius ut non transeat in gladium.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Increpat quoque per dolorem in lectulo, et omnia ossa ejus marcescere facit.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Abominabilis ei fit in vita sua panis, et animæ illius cibus ante desiderabilis.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Tabescet caro ejus, et ossa, quæ tecta fuerant, nudabuntur.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Appropinquavit corruptioni anima ejus, et vita illius mortiferis.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Si fuerit pro eo angelus loquens, unus de millibus, ut annuntiet hominis æquitatem,

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: miserebitur ejus, et dicet : Libera eum, ut non descendat in corruptionem : inveni in quo ei propitier.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Consumpta est caro ejus a suppliciis : revertatur ad dies adolescentiæ suæ.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Deprecabitur Deum, et placabilis ei erit : et videbit faciem ejus in jubilo, et reddet homini justitiam suam.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Respiciet homines, et dicet : Peccavi, et vere deliqui, et ut eram dignus, non recepi.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Liberavit animam suam, ne pergeret in interitum, sed vivens lucem videret.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Ecce hæc omnia operatur Deus tribus vicibus per singulos,

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: ut revocet animas eorum a corruptione, et illuminet luce viventium.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Attende, Job, et audi me : et tace, dum ego loquor.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Si autem habes quod loquaris, responde mihi : loquere, volo enim te apparere justum.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Quod si non habes, audi me : tace, et docebo te sapientiam.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Pronuntians itaque Eliu, etiam hæc locutus est :

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Audite, sapientes, verba mea : et eruditi, auscultate me.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Auris enim verba probat, et guttur escas gustu dijudicat.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Judicium eligamus nobis, et inter nos videamus quid sit melius.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Quia dixit Job : Justus sum, et Deus subvertit judicium meum.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: In judicando enim me mendacium est : violenta sagitta mea absque ullo peccato.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Quis est vir ut est Job, qui bibit subsannationem quasi aquam :

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: qui graditur cum operantibus iniquitatem, et ambulat cum viris impiis ?

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Dixit enim : Non placebit vir Deo, etiam si cucurrerit cum eo.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Ideo, viri cordati, audite me : absit a Deo impietas, et ab Omnipotente iniquitas.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Opus enim hominis reddet ei, et juxta vias singulorum restituet eis.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Vere enim Deus non condemnabit frustra, nec Omnipotens subvertet judicium.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Quem constituit alium super terram ? aut quem posuit super orbem quem fabricatus est ?

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Si direxerit ad eum cor suum, spiritum illius et flatum ad se trahet.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Deficiet omnis caro simul, et homo in cinerem revertetur.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Si habes ergo intellectum, audi quod dicitur, et ausculta vocem eloquii mei :

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: numquid qui non amat judicium sanari potest ? et quomodo tu eum qui justus est in tantum condemnas ?

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Qui dicit regi : Apostata ; qui vocat duces impios ;

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: qui non accipit personas principum, nec cognovit tyrannum cum disceptaret contra pauperem : opus enim manuum ejus sunt universi.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Subito morientur, et in media nocte turbabuntur populi : et pertransibunt, et auferent violentum absque manu.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Oculi enim ejus super vias hominum, et omnes gressus eorum considerat.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Non sunt tenebræ, et non est umbra mortis, ut abscondantur ibi qui operantur iniquitatem,

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: neque enim ultra in hominis potestate est, ut veniat ad Deum in judicium.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Conteret multos, et innumerabiles, et stare faciet alios pro eis.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Novit enim opera eorum, et idcirco inducet noctem, et conterentur.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Quasi impios percussit eos in loco videntium :

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: qui quasi de industria recesserunt ab eo, et omnes vias ejus intelligere noluerunt :

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: ut pervenire facerent ad eum clamorem egeni, et audiret vocem pauperum.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Ipso enim concedente pacem, quis est qui condemnet ? ex quo absconderit vultum, quis est qui contempletur eum, et super gentes, et super omnes homines ?

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Qui regnare facit hominem hypocritam propter peccata populi.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Quia ergo ego locutus sum ad Deum, te quoque non prohibebo.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Si erravi, tu doce me ; si iniquitatem locutus sum, ultra non addam.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Numquid a te Deus expetit eam, quia displicuit tibi ? tu enim cœpisti loqui, et non ego : quod si quid nosti melius, loquere.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Viri intelligentes loquantur mihi, et vir sapiens audiat me.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Job autem stulte locutus est, et verba illius non sonant disciplinam.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Pater mi, probetur Job usque ad finem : ne desinas ab homine iniquitatis :

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: quia addit super peccata sua blasphemiam, inter nos interim constringatur : et tunc ad judicium provocet sermonibus suis Deum.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Igitur Eliu hæc rursum locutus est :

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Numquid æqua tibi videtur tua cogitatio, ut diceres : Justior sum Deo ?

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Dixisti enim : Non tibi placet quod rectum est : vel quid tibi proderit, si ego peccavero ?

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Itaque ego respondebo sermonibus tuis, et amicis tuis tecum.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Suspice cælum, et intuere : et contemplare æthera quod altior te sit.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Si peccaveris, quid ei nocebis ? et si multiplicatæ fuerint iniquitates tuæ, quid facies contra eum ?

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Porro si juste egeris, quid donabis ei ? aut quid de manu tua accipiet ?

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Homini qui similis tui est, nocebit impietas tua : et filium hominis adjuvabit justitia tua.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Propter multitudinem calumniatorum clamabunt, et ejulabunt propter vim brachii tyrannorum.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Et non dixit : Ubi est Deus qui fecit me, qui dedit carmina in nocte ;

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: qui docet nos super jumenta terræ, et super volucres cæli erudit nos ?

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Ibi clamabunt, et non exaudiet, propter superbiam malorum.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Non ergo frustra audiet Deus, et Omnipotens causas singulorum intuebitur.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Etiam cum dixeris : Non considerat : judicare coram illo, et expecta eum.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Nunc enim non infert furorem suum, nec ulciscitur scelus valde.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Ergo Job frustra aperit os suum, et absque scientia verba multiplicat.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Addens quoque Eliu, hæc locutus est :

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Sustine me paululum, et indicabo tibi : adhuc enim habeo quod pro Deo loquar.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Repetam scientiam meam a principio, et operatorem meum probabo justum.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Vere enim absque mendacio sermones mei, et perfecta scientia probabitur tibi.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Deus potentes non abjicit, cum et ipse sit potens :

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: sed non salvat impios, et judicium pauperibus tribuit.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Non auferet a justo oculos suos : et reges in solio collocat in perpetuum, et illi eriguntur.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Et si fuerint in catenis, et vinciantur funibus paupertatis,

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: indicabit eis opera eorum, et scelera eorum, quia violenti fuerunt.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Revelabit quoque aurem eorum, ut corripiat : et loquetur, ut revertantur ab iniquitate.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Si audierint et observaverint, complebunt dies suos in bono, et annos suos in gloria :

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: si autem non audierint, transibunt per gladium, et consumentur in stultitia.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Simulatores et callidi provocant iram Dei, neque clamabunt cum vincti fuerint.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Morietur in tempestate anima eorum, et vita eorum inter effeminatos.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Eripiet de angustia sua pauperem, et revelabit in tribulatione aurem ejus.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Igitur salvabit te de ore angusto latissime, et non habente fundamentum subter se : requies autem mensæ tuæ erit plena pinguedine.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Causa tua quasi impii judicata est : causam judiciumque recipies.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Non te ergo superet ira ut aliquem opprimas : nec multitudo donorum inclinet te.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Depone magnitudinem tuam absque tribulatione, et omnes robustos fortitudine.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Ne protrahas noctem, ut ascendant populi pro eis.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Cave ne declines ad iniquitatem : hanc enim cœpisti sequi post miseriam.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Ecce Deus excelsus in fortitudine sua, et nullus ei similis in legislatoribus.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Quis poterit scrutari vias ejus ? aut quis potest ei dicere : Operatus es iniquitatem ?

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Memento quod ignores opus ejus, de quo cecinerunt viri.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Omnes homines vident eum : unusquisque intuetur procul.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Ecce Deus magnus vincens scientiam nostram : numerus annorum ejus inæstimabilis.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Qui aufert stillas pluviæ, et effundit imbres ad instar gurgitum,

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: qui de nubibus fluunt quæ prætexunt cuncta desuper.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Si voluerit extendere nubes quasi tentorium suum,

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: et fulgurare lumine suo desuper, cardines quoque maris operiet.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Per hæc enim judicat populos, et dat escas multis mortalibus.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: In manibus abscondit lucem, et præcepit ei ut rursus adveniat.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Annuntiat de ea amico suo, quod possessio ejus sit, et ad eam possit ascendere.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Super hoc expavit cor meum, et emotum est de loco suo.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Audite auditionem in terrore vocis ejus, et sonum de ore illius procedentem.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Subter omnes cælos ipse considerat, et lumen illius super terminos terræ.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Post eum rugiet sonitus ; tonabit voce magnitudinis suæ : et non investigabitur, cum audita fuerit vox ejus.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Tonabit Deus in voce sua mirabiliter, qui facit magna et inscrutabilia ;

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: qui præcipit nivi ut descendat in terram, et hiemis pluviis, et imbri fortitudinis suæ ;

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: qui in manu omnium hominum signat, ut noverint singuli opera sua.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Ingredietur bestia latibulum, et in antro suo morabitur.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Ab interioribus egredietur tempestas, et ab Arcturo frigus.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Flante Deo, concrescit gelu, et rursum latissimæ funduntur aquæ.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Frumentum desiderat nubes, et nubes spargunt lumen suum.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Quæ lustrant per circuitum, quocumque eas voluntas gubernantis duxerit, ad omne quod præceperit illis super faciem orbis terrarum :

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: sive in una tribu, sive in terra sua, sive in quocumque loco misericordiæ suæ eas jusserit inveniri.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Ausculta hæc, Job : sta, et considera mirabilia Dei.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Numquid scis quando præceperit Deus pluviis, ut ostenderent lucem nubium ejus ?

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Numquid nosti semitas nubium magnas, et perfectas scientias ?

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Nonne vestimenta tua calida sunt, cum perflata fuerit terra austro ?

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Tu forsitan cum eo fabricatus es cælos, qui solidissimi quasi ære fusi sunt.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Ostende nobis quid dicamus illi : nos quippe involvimur tenebris.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Quis narrabit ei quæ loquor ? etiam si locutus fuerit homo, devorabitur.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: At nunc non vident lucem : subito aër cogetur in nubes, et ventus transiens fugabit eas.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Ab aquilone aurum venit, et ad Deum formidolosa laudatio.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Digne eum invenire non possumus : magnus fortitudine, et judicio, et justitia : et enarrari non potest.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Ideo timebunt eum viri, et non audebunt contemplari omnes qui sibi videntur esse sapientes.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Respondens autem Dominus Job de turbine, dixit :

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Quis est iste involvens sententias sermonibus imperitis ?

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Accinge sicut vir lumbos tuos : interrogabo te, et responde mihi.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Ubi eras quando ponebam fundamenta terræ ? indica mihi, si habes intelligentiam.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Quis posuit mensuras ejus, si nosti ? vel quis tetendit super eam lineam ?

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Super quo bases illius solidatæ sunt ? aut quis demisit lapidem angularem ejus,

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: cum me laudarent simul astra matutina, et jubilarent omnes filii Dei ?

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Quis conclusit ostiis mare, quando erumpebat quasi de vulva procedens ;

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: cum ponerem nubem vestimentum ejus, et caligine illud quasi pannis infantiæ obvolverem ?

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Circumdedi illud terminis meis, et posui vectem et ostia,

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: et dixi : Usque huc venies, et non procedes amplius, et hic confringes tumentes fluctus tuos.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Numquid post ortum tuum præcepisti diluculo, et ostendisti auroræ locum suum ?

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Et tenuisti concutiens extrema terræ, et excussisti impios ex ea ?

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Restituetur ut lutum signaculum, et stabit sicut vestimentum :

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: auferetur ab impiis lux sua, et brachium excelsum confringetur.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Numquid ingressus es profunda maris, et in novissimis abyssi deambulasti ?

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Numquid apertæ sunt tibi portæ mortis, et ostia tenebrosa vidisti ?

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Numquid considerasti latitudinem terræ ? indica mihi, si nosti, omnia :

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: in qua via lux habitet, et tenebrarum quis locus sit :

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: ut ducas unumquodque ad terminos suos, et intelligas semitas domus ejus.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Sciebas tunc quod nasciturus esses, et numerum dierum tuorum noveras ?

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Numquid ingressus es thesauros nivis, aut thesauros grandinis aspexisti,

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: quæ præparavi in tempus hostis, in diem pugnæ et belli ?

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Per quam viam spargitur lux, dividitur æstus super terram ?

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Quis dedit vehementissimo imbri cursum, et viam sonantis tonitrui,

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: ut plueret super terram absque homine in deserto, ubi nullus mortalium commoratur ;

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: ut impleret inviam et desolatam, et produceret herbas virentes ?

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Quis est pluviæ pater ? vel quis genuit stillas roris ?

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: De cujus utero egressa est glacies ? et gelu de cælo quis genuit ?

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: In similitudinem lapidis aquæ durantur, et superficies abyssi constringitur.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Numquid conjungere valebis micantes stellas Pleiadas, aut gyrum Arcturi poteris dissipare ?

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Numquid producis luciferum in tempore suo, et vesperum super filios terræ consurgere facis ?

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Numquid nosti ordinem cæli, et pones rationem ejus in terra ?

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Numquid elevabis in nebula vocem tuam, et impetus aquarum operiet te ?

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Numquid mittes fulgura, et ibunt, et revertentia dicent tibi : Adsumus ?

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Quis posuit in visceribus hominis sapientiam ? vel quis dedit gallo intelligentiam ?

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Quis enarrabit cælorum rationem ? et concentum cæli quis dormire faciet ?

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Quando fundebatur pulvis in terra, et glebæ compingebantur ?

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Numquid capies leænæ prædam, et animam catulorum ejus implebis,

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: quando cubant in antris, et in specubus insidiantur ?

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Quis præparat corvo escam suam, quando pulli ejus clamant ad Deum, vagantes, eo quod non habeant cibos ?

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Numquid nosti tempus partus ibicum in petris, vel parturientes cervas observasti ?

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Dinumerasti menses conceptus earum, et scisti tempus partus earum ?

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Incurvantur ad fœtum, et pariunt, et rugitus emittunt.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Separantur filii earum, et pergunt ad pastum : egrediuntur, et non revertuntur ad eas.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Quis dimisit onagrum liberum, et vincula ejus quis solvit ?

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: cui dedi in solitudine domum, et tabernacula ejus in terra salsuginis.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Contemnit multitudinem civitatis : clamorem exactoris non audit.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Circumspicit montes pascuæ suæ, et virentia quæque perquirit.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Numquid volet rhinoceros servire tibi, aut morabitur ad præsepe tuum ?

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Numquid alligabis rhinocerota ad arandum loro tuo, aut confringet glebas vallium post te ?

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Numquid fiduciam habebis in magna fortitudine ejus, et derelinques ei labores tuos ?

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Numquid credes illi quod sementem reddat tibi, et aream tuam congreget ?

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Penna struthionis similis est pennis herodii et accipitris.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Quando derelinquit ova sua in terra, tu forsitan in pulvere calefacies ea ?

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Obliviscitur quod pes conculcet ea, aut bestia agri conterat.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Duratur ad filios suos, quasi non sint sui : frustra laboravit, nullo timore cogente.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Privavit enim eam Deus sapientia, nec dedit illi intelligentiam.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Cum tempus fuerit, in altum alas erigit : deridet equum et ascensorem ejus.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Numquid præbebis equo fortitudinem, aut circumdabis collo ejus hinnitum ?

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Numquid suscitabis eum quasi locustas ? gloria narium ejus terror.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Terram ungula fodit ; exultat audacter : in occursum pergit armatis.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Contemnit pavorem, nec cedit gladio.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Super ipsum sonabit pharetra ; vibrabit hasta et clypeus :

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: fervens et fremens sorbet terram, nec reputat tubæ sonare clangorem.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Ubi audierit buccinam, dicit : Vah ! procul odoratur bellum : exhortationem ducum, et ululatum exercitus.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Numquid per sapientiam tuam plumescit accipiter, expandens alas suas ad austrum ?

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Numquid ad præceptum tuum elevabitur aquila, et in arduis ponet nidum suum ?

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: In petris manet, et in præruptis silicibus commoratur, atque inaccessis rupibus.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Inde contemplatur escam, et de longe oculi ejus prospiciunt.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Pulli ejus lambent sanguinem : et ubicumque cadaver fuerit, statim adest.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Et adjecit Dominus, et locutus est ad Job :

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Numquid qui contendit cum Deo, tam facile conquiescit ? utique qui arguit Deum, debet respondere ei.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Respondens autem Job Domino, dixit :

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Qui leviter locutus sum, respondere quid possum ? manum meam ponam super os meum.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Unum locutus sum, quod utinam non dixissem : et alterum, quibus ultra non addam.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Respondens autem Dominus Job de turbine, dixit :

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Accinge sicut vir lumbos tuos : interrogabo te, et indica mihi.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Numquid irritum facies judicium meum, et condemnabis me, ut tu justificeris ?

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Et si habes brachium sicut Deus ? et si voce simili tonas ?

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Circumda tibi decorem, et in sublime erigere, et esto gloriosus, et speciosis induere vestibus.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Disperge superbos in furore tuo, et respiciens omnem arrogantem humilia.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Respice cunctos superbos, et confunde eos, et contere impios in loco suo.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Absconde eos in pulvere simul, et facies eorum demerge in foveam.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Et ego confitebor quod salvare te possit dextera tua.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Ecce behemoth quem feci tecum, fœnum quasi bos comedet.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Fortitudo ejus in lumbis ejus, et virtus illius in umbilico ventris ejus.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Stringit caudam suam quasi cedrum ; nervi testiculorum ejus perplexi sunt.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Ossa ejus velut fistulæ æris ; cartilago illius quasi laminæ ferreæ.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Ipse est principium viarum Dei : qui fecit eum applicabit gladium ejus.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Huic montes herbas ferunt : omnes bestiæ agri ludent ibi.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Sub umbra dormit in secreto calami, et in locis humentibus.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Protegunt umbræ umbram ejus : circumdabunt eum salices torrentis.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Ecce absorbebit fluvium, et non mirabitur, et habet fiduciam quod influat Jordanis in os ejus.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: In oculis ejus quasi hamo capiet eum, et in sudibus perforabit nares ejus.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: An extrahere poteris Leviathan hamo, et fune ligabis linguam ejus ?

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Numquid pones circulum in naribus ejus, aut armilla perforabis maxillam ejus ?

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Numquid multiplicabit ad te preces, aut loquetur tibi mollia ?

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Numquid feriet tecum pactum, et accipies eum servum sempiternum ?

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Numquid illudes ei quasi avi, aut ligabis eum ancillis tuis ?

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Concident eum amici ? divident illum negotiatores ?

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Numquid implebis sagenas pelle ejus, et gurgustium piscium capite illius ?

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Pone super eum manum tuam : memento belli, nec ultra addas loqui.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Ecce spes ejus frustrabitur eum, et videntibus cunctis præcipitabitur.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Non quasi crudelis suscitabo eum : quis enim resistere potest vultui meo ?

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Quis ante dedit mihi, ut reddam ei ? omnia quæ sub cælo sunt, mea sunt.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Non parcam ei, et verbis potentibus, et ad deprecandum compositis.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Quis revelabit faciem indumenti ejus ? et in medium oris ejus quis intrabit ?

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Portas vultus ejus quis aperiet ? per gyrum dentium ejus formido.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Corpus illius quasi scuta fusilia, compactum squamis se prementibus.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Una uni conjungitur, et ne spiraculum quidem incedit per eas.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Una alteri adhærebit, et tenentes se nequaquam separabuntur.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Sternutatio ejus splendor ignis, et oculi ejus ut palpebræ diluculi.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: De ore ejus lampades procedunt, sicut tædæ ignis accensæ.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: De naribus ejus procedit fumus, sicut ollæ succensæ atque ferventis.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Halitus ejus prunas ardere facit, et flamma de ore ejus egreditur.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: In collo ejus morabitur fortitudo, et faciem ejus præcedit egestas.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Membra carnium ejus cohærentia sibi : mittet contra eum fulmina, et ad locum alium non ferentur.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Cor ejus indurabitur tamquam lapis, et stringetur quasi malleatoris incus.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Cum sublatus fuerit, timebunt angeli, et territi purgabuntur.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Cum apprehenderit eum gladius, subsistere non poterit, neque hasta, neque thorax :

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: reputabit enim quasi paleas ferrum, et quasi lignum putridum æs.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Non fugabit eum vir sagittarius : in stipulam versi sunt ei lapides fundæ.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Quasi stipulam æstimabit malleum, et deridebit vibrantem hastam.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Sub ipso erunt radii solis, et sternet sibi aurum quasi lutum.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Fervescere faciet quasi ollam profundum mare, et ponet quasi cum unguenta bulliunt.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Post eum lucebit semita : æstimabit abyssum quasi senescentem.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Non est super terram potestas quæ comparetur ei, qui factus est ut nullum timeret.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Omne sublime videt : ipse est rex super universos filios superbiæ.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Respondens autem Job Domino, dixit :

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Scio quia omnia potes, et nulla te latet cogitatio.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Quis est iste qui celat consilium absque scientia ? ideo insipienter locutus sum, et quæ ultra modum excederent scientiam meam.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Audi, et ego loquar : interrogabo te, et responde mihi.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Auditu auris audivi te : nunc autem oculus meus videt te.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Idcirco ipse me reprehendo, et ago pœnitentiam in favilla et cinere.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Postquam autem locutus est Dominus verba hæc ad Job, dixit ad Eliphaz Themanitem : Iratus est furor meus in te, et in duos amicos tuos, quoniam non estis locuti coram me rectum, sicut servus meus Job.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Sumite ergo vobis septem tauros et septem arietes, et ite ad servum meum Job, et offerte holocaustum pro vobis : Job autem servus meus orabit pro vobis. Faciem ejus suscipiam, ut non vobis imputetur stultitia : neque enim locuti estis ad me recta, sicut servus meus Job.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Abierunt ergo Eliphaz Themanites, et Baldad Suhites, et Sophar Naamathites, et fecerunt sicut locutus fuerat Dominus ad eos : et suscepit Dominus faciem Job.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Dominus quoque conversus est ad pœnitentiam Job, cum oraret ille pro amicis suis : et addidit Dominus omnia quæcumque fuerant Job, duplicia.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Venerunt autem ad eum omnes fratres sui, et universæ sorores suæ, et cuncti qui noverant eum prius, et comederunt cum eo panem in domo ejus : et moverunt super eum caput, et consolati sunt eum super omni malo quod intulerat Dominus super eum : et dederunt ei unusquisque ovem unam, et inaurem auream unam.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Dominus autem benedixit novissimis Job magis quam principio ejus : et facta sunt ei quatuordecim millia ovium, et sex millia camelorum, et mille juga boum, et mille asinæ.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Et fuerunt ei septem filii, et tres filiæ.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Et vocavit nomen unius Diem, et nomen secundæ Cassiam, et nomen tertiæ Cornustibii.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Non sunt autem inventæ mulieres speciosæ sicut filiæ Job in universa terra : deditque eis pater suus hæreditatem inter fratres earum.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Vixit autem Job post hæc centum quadraginta annis, et vidit filios suos, et filios filiorum suorum usque ad quartam generationem : et mortuus est senex, et plenus dierum.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)
